note: source.url resolves and is official OPSS/GOV.UK statutory guidance updated 2025-03-25. SI 2016/1101 confirmed on legislation.gov.uk; voltage scope 50-1000 V AC / 75-1500 V DC is correct. Mandatory status correct. BS EN IEC 62109-1/-2 are recognised UK designated standards providing presumption of conformity. Gap description (UK DoC, 10-year retention, UK-based AR) is accurate. Minor UNVERIFIED note in cn_common_equivalent about GB/T 37408 alignment with IEC 62109:2022 is legitimately flagged.

source.url (gov.uk UKCA/CE guidance) resolves; page confirmed live, published by Dept for Business and Trade, last updated 2026-04-07. CE marking confirmed accepted indefinitely in GB under SI 2024/696 (The Product Safety and Metrology etc. (Amendment) Regulations 2024) — SI number was [UNVERIFIED] in the fragment; it is SI 2024/696, in force 1 Oct 2024. Product Regulation and Metrology Act 2025 received Royal Assent 21 July 2025 (the fragment attributed the indefinite CE acceptance to that Act but it predates it; the correct instrument is SI 2024/696). The UKCA label-on-document flexibility to 2027-12-31 is confirmed. Windsor Framework / NI CE requirement is correct. Mandatory status correct.

source.url same as ukpv-safety-001 (EESR 2016 GOV.UK guidance) — confirmed live. SI 2016/1101 reg. 8 UK-based AR obligation is correctly described. The statement that an EU AR does not satisfy UK obligations is accurate post-Brexit. Mandatory status correct. UNVERIFIED note about Product Regulation and Metrology Act 2025 changes to AR definition is legitimately flagged — the Act received Royal Assent 21 July 2025 but detailed secondary legislation implementing any AR changes has not been confirmed here.

source.url (legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2016/1091) confirmed live and correct for EMC Regs 2016. SI number 2016/1091 verified. Standards BS EN IEC 61000-6-2:2019 and 61000-6-4:2019 are the correct UK designated standards. However, the gap section contains a material factual error: it states 'CE marking is not accepted in GB from 1 Jan 2025 for most categories.' This is incorrect. SI 2024/696 (Product Safety and Metrology etc. (Amendment) Regulations 2024, in force 1 Oct 2024) extended CE marking recognition indefinitely for GB, including EMC. The text should be corrected to reflect that CE continues to be accepted alongside UKCA with no withdrawal deadline published. This error is potentially misleading to users. Mandatory status (Mandatory under SI 2016/1091) is correct.

source.url (legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2016/1091) confirmed. SI 2016/1091 is the correct instrument. BS EN IEC 61000-3-2 and 61000-3-12 are correctly identified as the applicable standards. Mandatory status is correct. The UNVERIFIED flags on current designation status and GB 17625.1 / GB/T 17625.8 version alignment are appropriate cautions. The 16 A/phase boundary between 61000-3-2 and 61000-3-12 is accurate.

source.url (energynetworks.org resource-library?Download=true&resourceId=11895) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden — the direct-download parametric link is not publicly accessible. Replace with the canonical ENA G98/G99 publications page: https://www.energynetworks.org/electricity/engineering/distributed-generation/engineering-recommendation-g99.html or the ENA publications index. Standards G98/G99 and their applicability are confirmed correct. However, the versions cited (G98 Issue 1 Amendment 7 (2023) and G99 Issue 1 Amendment 6 (2023)) are inaccurate: Amendment 7 to G98 was issued in 2022, Amendment 6 to G99 was issued in 2020. Both have since been superseded by Issue 2 (published March 2025). The mandatory status is correct (required by all GB DNOs). The CN equivalent description (GB/T 19964, NB/T 32004) and gap analysis are accurate.

source.url (energynetworks.org/industry-hub/resource-library/type-test-register) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden. The ENA Type Test Register is confirmed to exist but is accessed via https://www.ena-eng.org/gen-ttr/ or https://connect-direct.energynetworks.org/device-databases/search-gen — update source.url to one of these. Standards references (G98/G99 Appendix A type test requirements) are accurate. Mandatory status (ENA register listing prerequisite for DNO connection) is correct. CN equivalent (CGC/CQC not recognised by ENA) is accurate.

source.url (energynetworks.org/industry-hub/connections-smart-tool) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden — the ENA CST page may be behind authentication or have moved. A public-facing entry point for G99 DNO connection applications is https://www.energynetworks.org/electricity/engineering/distributed-generation/engineering-recommendation-g99.html — update source.url. The description of G99 process (Type A notification vs Type B/C/D full application based on capacity) is correct. Electricity Act 1989 Section 16 citation is plausible but should be noted as the provision creating connection rights; the obligation to have a Connection Agreement before energising flows from G99 and the licences, not directly from s.16. Mandatory status is correct.

source.url (energynetworks.org resource-library?Download=true&resourceId=14175) returns HTTP 403 Forbidden. Replace with https://www.energynetworks.org/electricity/engineering/distributed-generation/engineering-recommendation-g100.html. G100 version cited in the fragment ('Issue 1 (2020, updated 2022)') is outdated — the current version is G100 Issue 2 Amendment 2 (2023), available at energynetworks.org. Standards description and mandatory_status (conditional mandatory where DNO imposes export-limitation condition) are correct. CN equivalent description (no G100 equivalent, Chinese inverters use factory-set protocols) is accurate.

source.url (gov.uk/guidance/placing-ukca-or-ce-marked-products-on-the-market-in-great-britain) confirmed live, published by Dept for Business and Trade, last updated 2026-04-07. CE marking indefinitely accepted under SI 2024/696 confirmed. The statement that CE marking is 'the practical minimum for GB' and no published withdrawal deadline is accurate. EESR 2016 / EMC 2016 as primary UK product regulations for PV inverters (50-1000 V AC / 75-1500 V DC) is correct. Mandatory status correct. The Product Safety and Metrology (Amendment) Regulations 2024 reference is correct (SI 2024/696).

source.url (gov.uk/guidance/using-the-ukca-marking) confirmed live (redirects to the same UKCA/CE page, confirmed correct content). UK DoC content requirements cited (manufacturer address, product ID, UK legislation reference, designated standards, approved body details, signatory) align with EESR 2016 Schedule 3. 10-year technical file retention period is the standard requirement under EESR 2016 and EMC 2016 — confirmed. Mandatory status correct.

source.url (gov.uk/guidance/placing-ukca-or-ce-marked-products-on-the-market-in-great-britain) confirmed live. Economic operator hierarchy (manufacturer → UK AR → importer → distributor) under EESR 2016 regs 6-9 is correct. Labelling requirements (5 mm minimum mark height, name/address, UK AR or importer address) are confirmed by gov.uk guidance. Label-on-document flexibility to 2027-12-31 for UKCA is confirmed. UNVERIFIED note on whether a non-UK importer address suffices under indefinite CE recognition is legitimately flagged — this is an area of practical ambiguity in current UK guidance. Windsor Framework NI requirement is correct. Mandatory status correct.

source.url (legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2012/3032) confirmed live and correct. SI 2012/3032 and amending SI 2019/492 are correctly cited. The 10 restricted substances (Pb, Hg, Cd, Cr6+, PBB, PBDE, DEHP, BBP, DBP, DIBP) and 0.1%/0.01% thresholds are accurate. PV inverters (as electronic equipment) fall within scope of UK RoHS — note the RoHS exclusion for PV panels does not cover inverters. GB/T 26572-2011 as the CN equivalent is correct. Mandatory status correct. Administrative gaps (UK documentation, UKCA, diverging exemptions) are correctly described.

source.url (legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2013/3113) confirmed live and correct. WEEE Regs 2013 and amending SI 2019/428 correctly cited. Producer registration with WEEE PCS before first GB sale is correctly described as mandatory with criminal liability for non-compliance. Environment Agency (E/W), SEPA (Scotland), NIEA (NI) are the correct registration bodies. CN equivalent (State Council Order No. 551 fund-contribution scheme, no PCS equivalent) is accurate. Mandatory status correct.

source.url (hse.gov.uk/reach/) confirmed live as the official HSE UK REACH hub. UK REACH retained via EU (Withdrawal) Act 2018 and SI 2019/758 is correctly cited. SVHC notification threshold (>0.1% w/w AND >1 tonne/year total articles) is accurate. The note that UK REACH SVHC Candidate List diverges from EU ECHA post-Brexit is correct. Primary burden on GB importer, not CN manufacturer, is correctly stated. Mandatory status correct. UNVERIFIED note on IECSC scope interaction is appropriately flagged.

source.url (legislation.gov.uk/uksi/2017/1206) confirmed live and correct as the Radio Equipment Regulations 2017. SI 2017/1206 is the correct instrument. The three essential requirements (health and safety reg 6(1)(a), EMC reg 6(1)(b), spectrum reg 6(2)) are accurate. The statement that RER 2017 applies independently of and in addition to EESR/EMC Regs for wireless inverters is correct. SRRC type approval not accepted as RER 2017 conformity evidence is correct. The UNVERIFIED note about Retained EU Law (Revocation and Reform) Act 2023 effect on SI 2017/1206 is appropriately cautious — the operative text should be checked at legislation.gov.uk. Mandatory_status 'conditional' is correct (only applies if wireless module present).

source.url (gov.uk/guidance/using-the-ukca-marking) confirmed live. The listed BS EN standards (300 328, 301 893, 300 220 series, 301 489-1, 301 489-17, 62311/IEC 62311) are the correct UK designated standard equivalents for the radio technology types described. The post-Brexit version-divergence caveat is appropriately flagged (UNVERIFIED note on exact version numbers). The gap description (SRRC not accepted, EU ETSI-cited reports require fresh UK DoC, UK DoC must cite SI 2017/1206) is accurate. Mandatory_status 'conditional' is correct.
